name: Al Maktoum

name: Al Mualla

originStory: The Al Ali tribe originated in Nejd and moved from the island of Sinniyah to the mainland after water supplies on the island were exhausted. Sheikh Rashid bin Majid Al Mualla was the first known head when they settled at Umm Al Quwain.

description: The ruling royal family of Umm Al Quwain, one of the seven emirates that together comprise the United Arab Emirates (UAE). The family was traditionally at the head of the Al Ali tribe.

Various prominent individuals bearing this name are found across different Gulf countries in fields including politics, academia, sports, and human rights.

name: Al Nuaimi
currentHead: Sheikh Humaid bin Rashid Al Nuaimi III
originStory: Sheikh Rashid bin Humaid Al Nuaimi and fifty followers took the coastal settlement of Ajman from members of the Al Bu Shamis tribe in a short conflict. Rule was established when the Ajman fort fell to Rashid's followers in 1816 or 1817.
legitimacyBasis: Military conquest of coastal settlement from Al Bu Shamis tribe, endorsed by Sheikh Sultan bin Saqr Al Qasimi
description: The ruling royal family of Ajman, one of the seven emirates that comprise the United Arab Emirates. The family name is derived from the Na'im, a major tribal confederation.

name: Al Saud (House of Saud)
originStory: Descended from Banu Hanifa, connected to the Anizzah confederation. Adnanite lineage.

name: Al Sharqi
originStory: Derives from the Sharqiyin tribe, long the dominant traditionally Ghafiri tribe along the North East coast of the Trucial States. Originally dependents of Sharjah who made several attempts to secede and declare independence, finally managing this from 1901 onwards and gaining British recognition as a Trucial State in 1952
legitimacyBasis: British recognition as Trucial State in 1952, practical independence from 1901
description: The ruling royal family of Fujairah, one of the seven emirates that comprise the United Arab Emirates

name: Al Thani
originStory: Branch of Maadhid tribe, claim descent from Bani Tamim. Originally from Jabrin oasis. Migration: Jabrin → south Qatar → Zubarah → Fuwairit → Doha (1848-1850).
description: Ruling family of Qatar, with members involved in cultural patronage and art collection

notableFigures.name: Sheikh Khalifa bin Hamad al-Thani
notableFigures.title: Emir of Qatar
notableFigures.roleDescription: Deposed cousin Ahmad in 1972. Was himself deposed by his own son Hamad in 1995.

notableFigures.name: Sheikh Ahmad bin Ali al-Thani
notableFigures.title: Emir of Qatar
notableFigures.roleDescription: Deposed by his cousin Khalifa in 1972 coup

notableFigures.name: Sheikh Hamad bin Abdullah Al Thani
notableFigures.nameAr: الشيخ حمد بن عبد الله آل ثاني
notableFigures.title: Sheikh
notableFigures.roleDescription: First cousin of the Emir of Qatar and art collector
notableFigures.significance: Assembled the Al Thani Collection, one of the world's notable art collections representing civilizations from around the world

name: Al-Buainain
rulesOver: Various settlements including Jubail and parts of Al Wakrah
originStory: One of several bedouin tribes that moved to Bahrain in 1783 after the Al Khalifa conquered the island. They established the town of Jubail on the Hasa coast following dissatisfaction in Qatar and helped build up Al Wakrah in Qatar.
description: A clan of the Bani Tamim tribe divided into four main branches: al-Khater, al-Muhammad, al-Ali and Al-Nuwasir. Based primarily in Eastern Province of Saudi Arabia, Qatar, United Arab Emirates and Bahrain. Historically involved in the pearl diving industry.

notableFigures.name: Mohammed Al-Buainain
notableFigures.title: Politician, Engineer, and Pilot
notableFigures.roleDescription: Bahraini politician sworn into the Council of Representatives on December 12, 2018 for the eighth district in the Southern Governorate
notableFigures.significance: Won 3,984 votes (63.28%) in the first round of elections in 2018. Has educational background in aviation sciences and engineering.

name: Al-Fadhil
rulesOver: Bedouin tribes of northern Syria, territories between Homs in the west and Qal'at Ja'bar to east, and between Euphrates valley in the north to central Arabia in the south
originStory: Descended from Fadl ibn Rabi'ah, who was a descendant of the Banu Tayy through his ancestor, Mufarrij al-Jarrah. The Banu Rabi'ah were descendants of the 10th-century Jarrahid rulers of Palestine. Rose to prominence by assisting the Burids and Zengids against the Crusaders.
legitimacyBasis: Hereditary appointment to office of Amir al-ʿarab by Mamluk sultans, descent from Jarrahid rulers of Palestine
description: Al Fadl were an Arab tribe that dominated the Syrian Desert and steppe during the Middle Ages, descended from the Banu Tayy through Fadl ibn Rabi'ah. They served as hereditary holders of the office of Amir al-ʿarab under various rulers and controlled extensive territories in Syria and northern Arabia.

notableFigures.name: Fadl ibn Rabi'ah
notableFigures.title: Emir
notableFigures.roleDescription: Progenitor of the Al Fadl tribe, noted as an emir by 1107
notableFigures.significance: Founder of the Al Fadl branch, provided mounted auxiliary troops for Burid and Zengid rulers against Crusaders

notableFigures.name: Haditha
notableFigures.title: Amir al-ʿarab
notableFigures.roleDescription: Grandson of Fadl ibn Rabi'ah, first to hold the office of amir al-ʿarab
notableFigures.significance: Appointed as commander of the Bedouin by Sultan al-Adil, establishing Al Fadl authority over northern Syrian tribes

notableFigures.name: Isa ibn Muhanna
notableFigures.title: Emir
notableFigures.roleDescription: Made the Al Fadl hereditary holders of the office of amir al-ʿarab by Mamluk sultans
notableFigures.significance: Established hereditary nature of Al Fadl leadership, given substantial iqtaʿat (fiefs) in Salamiyah and Palmyra

notableFigures.name: Muhanna
notableFigures.title: Emir
notableFigures.roleDescription: Son and successor of Isa ibn Muhanna
notableFigures.significance: Vacillated between Mamluks and Mongols but generally favored by Sultan an-Nasir Muhammad

notableFigures.name: Fadl
notableFigures.title: Emir
notableFigures.roleDescription: Son and successor of Isa ibn Muhanna, brother of Muhanna
notableFigures.significance: Co-ruler with his brother Muhanna, vacillated between Mamluks and Mongols

name: Al-Mannai
originStory: Originated from Banu Tamim tribe, with history in pearl trading going back to 1824 when Salem Bin Darwish Al Mannai established the pearl trading business in Bahrain
description: An Arab tribe that originated from Banu Tamim, based primarily in the Arab states of the Persian Gulf. Known for natural pearl trading in Bahrain, with traditional home in Galali village. Has established major commercial enterprises including Mannai Corporation in Qatar.

notableFigures.title: Head of the family
notableFigures.roleDescription: Established the pearl trading business in Bahrain

notableFigures.significance: Leader of Qatar section with 70-80 fighting men

name: Al-Noaimi
currentHead: Sheikh Humaid bin Rashid Al Nuaimi III
originStory: The foundation of Ajman under Al Nuaimi rule took place when Sheikh Rashid bin Humaid Al Nuaimi and fifty of his followers took the coastal settlement of Ajman from members of the Al Bu Shamis tribe in a short conflict. The rule was endorsed by the Sheikh of neighbouring Sharjah and Ras Al Khaimah in 1816 or 1817.
legitimacyBasis: Military conquest endorsed by neighbouring powerful sheikhs
description: The ruling royal family of Ajman, one of the seven emirates that comprise the United Arab Emirates (UAE). The family name is derived from the singular of 'Na'im', which is a major tribal confederation.

name: Al-Thawadi

description: Qatari family with members serving in diplomatic and government positions

notableFigures.name: Ali al-Thawadi
notableFigures.title: Minister
notableFigures.roleDescription: Qatari minister, diplomat, and former chief of staff to Mohammed bin Hamad Al Thani
notableFigures.significance: Deputy chief executive for Qatar's 2022 FIFA World Cup bid, involved in Gaza-Israel mediation, currently serves as Minister at the Prime Minister's Office for strategy affairs and Qatari representative to the Gaza Executive Board

name: Cohen
originStory: The Cohen lineage traces back to the priests who served in the Temple of Jerusalem. Aaron, the brother of Moses, is generally regarded as the patriarch of the lineage and the first Kohen. The hereditary caste descending from these priests is collectively known as the Kohanim.
legitimacyBasis: Religious hereditary caste based on patrilineal descent from Temple priests, with Aaron as the founding patriarch
description: A hereditary priestly caste in Judaism descending from the Temple priests of Jerusalem, collectively known as the Kohanim. The surname indicates patrilineal descent from Temple priests, with Aaron (brother of Moses) regarded as the patriarch of the lineage and first Kohen.

notableFigures.name: Leonard Norman Cohen
notableFigures.title: Canadian songwriter, singer, poet, and novelist
notableFigures.roleDescription: Renowned Canadian artist who became one of the most influential singer-songwriters of his generation
notableFigures.significance: Inducted into multiple halls of fame including Rock and Roll Hall of Fame, received Order of Canada, created iconic songs like 'Hallelujah', named 103rd-greatest singer by Rolling Stone in 2023

notableFigures.name: Aaron
notableFigures.title: First High Priest
notableFigures.roleDescription: Brother of Moses and first Kohen, patriarch of the priestly lineage
notableFigures.significance: Regarded as the founding patriarch of the Cohen/Kohanim lineage and first High Priest

notableFigures.name: Lyon Cohen
notableFigures.title: Canadian Jewish Congress founding president
notableFigures.roleDescription: Leonard Cohen's paternal grandfather and founding president of Canadian Jewish Congress
notableFigures.significance: Founding president of Canadian Jewish Congress, emigrated from Congress Poland to Canada

name: House of Sheikh Saeed bin Hamad Al Qasimi
Khedouri
originStory: The Khedouri family arrived in Bahrain as part of Jewish settlement in the late 1880s, establishing themselves as importers of textiles and linens
description: Jewish merchant family in Bahrain involved in importing tablecloths and linens, with third-generation members in Bahrain and connections to banking through related branches

notableFigures.name: Nancy Khedouri
notableFigures.title: Member of National Assembly
notableFigures.roleDescription: Bahraini politician, businesswoman and author, member of National Assembly since 2010
notableFigures.significance: First Jewish woman in Bahraini parliament, documented history of Bahraini Jews, works on Israel-Bahrain relations

notableFigures.name: Khedouri Aboody Zilkha
notableFigures.title: Banker and Financier
notableFigures.roleDescription: Iraqi-Jewish banker who founded Zilkha Bank and expanded internationally
notableFigures.significance: Built major banking empire across Middle East, Europe and Americas; emigrated to New York in 1941-42

name: Nonoo
originStory: The Nonoo family originated in Iraq. Houda's grandfather Ibrahim left Baghdad in 1888 and started a financial business in Bahrain, establishing the family's presence in the kingdom.
description: A Jewish business family originally from Iraq that immigrated to Bahrain over a century ago. The family has been prominent in business and politics in Bahrain, with members serving in diplomatic and governmental roles.

notableFigures.name: Ebrahim Daoud Nonoo
notableFigures.title: Businessman and Politician
notableFigures.roleDescription: Bahraini businessman and politician appointed to parliament in 2000
notableFigures.significance: Member of the Bahraini parliament, continuing the family's political involvement

notableFigures.name: Misha Nonoo
notableFigures.title: Fashion Designer
notableFigures.roleDescription: US-based British-Bahraini fashion designer with eponymous women's ready-to-wear line
notableFigures.significance: Notable fashion designer, Forbes 30 Under 30 recipient, collaborated with Meghan Duchess of Sussex

notableFigures.name: Ibrahim Nonoo
notableFigures.title: Family Patriarch
notableFigures.roleDescription: Grandfather who established the family in Bahrain by starting a financial business
notableFigures.significance: Founded the family's presence in Bahrain in 1888, establishing their business legacy

notableFigures.name: Abraham Nonoo
notableFigures.title: Municipal Councilor
notableFigures.roleDescription: Served as member of Manama Municipality in 1934
notableFigures.significance: First family member to enter Bahraini politics, served on the first elected municipal body in Bahrain

notableFigures.name: Houda Ezra Ebrahim Nonoo
notableFigures.title: Houda Ezra Ebrahim Nonoo
notableFigures.roleDescription: Bahraini politician and diplomat, first Jewish ambassador of Bahrain and first female Bahraini ambassador to the United States
notableFigures.significance: First Jew and third woman to be appointed ambassador of Bahrain. First Jewish ambassador of any Middle Eastern Arab country. First female Bahraini ambassador to the United States. Businesswoman and human rights advocate.

name: Rouben
originStory: Armenian family name, with documented members tracing to Tiflis (modern Tbilisi, Georgia) in the late 19th century
description: The Rouben family appears to be an Armenian family name with notable members in the arts and academia, including film director Rouben Mamoulian and mathematician Rouben V. Ambartzumian. The name is of Armenian origin.

notableFigures.name: Rouben Zachary Mamoulian
notableFigures.title: Film and Theater Director
notableFigures.roleDescription: Armenian-American film and theater director, responsible for acclaimed original stagings of Oklahoma! and Carousel, and innovative early sound films
notableFigures.significance: Pioneer in cinema and theater, directed landmark productions and films including Dr. Jekyll and Mr. Hyde (1931), first three-strip Technicolor film Becky Sharp (1935)

notableFigures.name: Rouben V. Ambartzumian
notableFigures.title: Mathematician and Academician
notableFigures.roleDescription: Armenian mathematician and Academician of National Academy of Sciences of Armenia, specialist in Stochastic Geometry and Integral Geometry
notableFigures.significance: Created new branch of combinatorial integral geometry, solved classical problems including Buffon Sylvester problem and Hilbert's fourth problem in 1976, holder of Rollo Davidson Prize 1982
